rule.name: Insecure Local Storage of Secrets
rule.rule_desc: Detects local persistence patterns where sensitive value handling should be verified.
rule.vuln_desc: Improper secret storage can expose credentials or tokens to local compromise.
rule.developer: Use secure storage APIs for secrets and avoid plaintext persistence in app preferences.
rule.reviewer: Inspect storage calls to ensure only non-sensitive values use preference stores. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: TLS Certificate Validation Bypass Callback
rule.rule_desc: Detects explicit certificate validation bypass in HTTP client configuration.
rule.vuln_desc: Bypassing certificate validation enables man-in-the-middle interception.
rule.developer: Use default certificate validation or robust pinning with proper fallback handling.
rule.reviewer: Verify no certificate callback unconditionally returns true. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: WebView JavaScript Enabled
rule.rule_desc: Detects potentially risky web view JavaScript configuration in Xamarin apps.
rule.vuln_desc: Untrusted content rendered with active scripting can lead to data exposure and injection-like abuse.
rule.developer: Restrict origins and disable JavaScript unless necessary.
rule.reviewer: Check WebView source restrictions and script execution requirements. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Xamarin Debug Build Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects debug build markers in Xamarin/mobile .NET code paths.
rule.vuln_desc: Debug builds can expose additional diagnostics and attack surface.
rule.developer: Ensure release builds disable debug behavior.
rule.reviewer: Review build configuration and conditional compilation paths. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Xamarin Certificate Validation Bypass
rule.rule_desc: Detects TLS certificate validation bypass patterns.
rule.vuln_desc: Bypassing TLS validation allows MITM interception attacks.
rule.developer: Remove bypass logic and enforce strict cert validation.
rule.reviewer: Audit all HTTP client handlers and global callbacks. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Xamarin Insecure HTTP Endpoint Usage
rule.rule_desc: Detects cleartext endpoint references in Xamarin apps.
rule.vuln_desc: Cleartext transport can leak sensitive information.
rule.developer: Use HTTPS endpoints only and enforce TLS policy.
rule.reviewer: Audit endpoint constants and networking client setup. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Xamarin WebView Script Bridge Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects webview/bridge execution points needing security review.
rule.vuln_desc: Unsafe bridge/content handling may permit script injection or data leakage.
rule.developer: Restrict untrusted content and minimize bridge capabilities.
rule.reviewer: Review webview sources, script execution, and bridge handlers. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Xamarin Secret Storage Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects secret-like keys written through app storage APIs.
rule.vuln_desc: Improper storage choices can leak credentials/tokens.
rule.developer: Use platform secure storage appropriately for sensitive values.
rule.reviewer: Review storage API usage and key naming for sensitive data. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Xamarin Hardcoded Secret Marker
rule.rule_desc: Detects likely hardcoded secret literals in Xamarin code/config.
rule.vuln_desc: Hardcoded credentials can be extracted from binaries/source.
rule.developer: Externalize secrets and rotate any exposed credentials.
rule.reviewer: Verify no production secrets are embedded in client code. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Android Backup Enabled
rule.rule_desc: Detects Android backup configuration that can expose local app data.
rule.vuln_desc: Backups may leak sensitive app data if not encrypted or excluded.
rule.developer: Disable backups for sensitive apps or explicitly exclude sensitive data.
rule.reviewer: Validate backup policy based on data sensitivity and compliance requirements. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: iOS Keychain Accessibility Too Broad
rule.rule_desc: Detects permissive keychain accessibility constants in iOS-specific Xamarin code.
rule.vuln_desc: Weak keychain accessibility can increase exposure of stored secrets.
rule.developer: Use stricter keychain accessibility constants aligned with runtime requirements.
rule.reviewer: Review keychain configuration and enforce least-privilege accessibility. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.
